Title: Modal Open Time - Few Locations
Preconditions:
- 1-5 pickup locations configured
- DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Performance tab
[ACT] Start performance recording
[ACT] Click BOPIS/pickup trigger to open modal
[ACT] Stop recording once modal is fully interactive
[WAIT] Wait for modal animation to complete
[ASSERT] Measure elapsed time from click to modal interactive state using performance.now()
Assertions:
[MATH] Modal open time < 500ms
[DOM] Modal overlay is visible and fully rendered
[STATE] Location list is populated (≥1 item visible)
[STATE] Animation completes without frame drops
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] No more than 1 API call fired to load location data
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during modal open
Failure_Signals: spinner visible >500ms; modal open time ≥500ms; console errors present

Title: Modal Open Time - Many Locations
Preconditions:
- 10-50 pickup locations configured
- DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Start performance recording in DevTools
[ACT] Click BOPIS/pickup trigger to open modal
[WAIT] Wait for location list and map to fully render
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Measure modal open duration using performance.now()
Assertions:
[MATH] Modal open time < 1000ms (1 second)
[DOM] Location list rendered with all available entries
[DOM] Map component rendered and interactive
[STATE] No loading spinner remaining after 1s
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] API response received within 800ms
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors or warnings
Failure_Signals: spinner visible >1s; map tiles blank after 1s; modal open time ≥1000ms

Title: Modal Open Time - High Volume
Preconditions:
- 50-100 pickup locations configured
- DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Start performance recording in DevTools
[ACT] Click BOPIS/pickup trigger to open modal
[WAIT] Wait for modal to reach interactive state
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Measure elapsed open time
[ASSERT] Inspect list for pagination controls or map for marker clustering
Assertions:
[MATH] Modal open time < 2000ms (2 seconds)
[DOM] Pagination controls present OR map markers are clustered (not individual pins per location)
[STATE] List is navigable and usable within the 2s threshold
[STATE] No blank or broken UI state
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Verify API request uses limit/offset params or returns clustered data
[CONSOLE] No errors; warn if single unbounded request returns 50-100 items without pagination
Failure_Signals: modal open time ≥2s; no pagination or clustering when >50 locations; console errors present

Title: Search Response Time
Preconditions:
- Pickup modal open and location list visible
- DevTools Network tab open to monitor API calls
Steps:
[ACT] Focus the search/filter input inside the pickup modal
[ACT] Type a search query character by character (use browser_type with delay)
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results to appear
[ASSERT] Measure time from last keypress to results update using performance.now()
[ACT] Clear input and repeat with two additional queries
[ASSERT] Count API requests fired during rapid typing to verify debouncing
Assertions:
[MATH] Results appear within 500ms of final keypress
[STATE] Result list updates to match query without full page reload
[STATE] No lag or freeze during typing
[NETWORK] Debouncing: ≤1 API call per distinct query (not per keystroke)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Monitor /api calls; confirm debounce delay ~300ms between last keystroke and API fire
[CONSOLE] No errors during search interaction
Failure_Signals: results delay >500ms; multiple API calls per keystroke (debounce not working); console errors

Title: Filter Response Time
Preconditions:
- Pickup modal open
- Multiple filter options visible (e.g., distance, availability)
- DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Start performance recording
[ACT] Apply a filter option (e.g., select a distance or availability filter)
[WAIT] Wait for location list to update
[WAIT] Wait for map to update marker positions/visibility
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Measure time from filter interaction to list and map update
Assertions:
[MATH] Filter applied and list updated within 500ms
[MATH] Map markers updated within 500ms
[STATE] List and map are in sync (same locations reflected in both)
[STATE] No visible stutter or blank state during transition
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Single API call triggered per filter change; no duplicate requests
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during filter interaction
Failure_Signals: filter update >500ms; list and map out of sync; duplicate API calls; console errors

Title: Map Interaction - Pan
Preconditions: - Map component visible and loaded inside pickup modal
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Performance tab and start recording
[ACT] Click and drag the map to pan in multiple directions (left, right, up, down)
[WAIT] Continue panning for at least 3 seconds
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Inspect frame rate data in the performance recording for pan duration
Assertions:
[MATH] Frame rate ≥ 60fps sustained during panning (no drops below 30fps)
[STATE] Map tiles load as panning proceeds; no persistent blank tile areas
[STATE] Map responds immediately to drag input without input lag
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during map pan interaction
Failure_Signals: frame rate drops below 30fps; blank map tiles >2s; jank or stuttering visible; console errors

Title: Map Interaction - Zoom
Preconditions: - Map component visible inside pickup modal
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Performance tab and start recording
[ACT] Zoom in using scroll wheel or pinch gesture (5 zoom levels in)
[ACT] Zoom back out (5 zoom levels out)
[ACT] Repeat zoom in/out cycle 3 times
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Inspect frame rate across the zoom interactions
Assertions:
[MATH] Frame rate ≥ 60fps during zoom operations
[STATE] Map tiles render at new zoom level within 1s; no persistent blank areas
[STATE] Zoom controls respond immediately (no input lag)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during zoom
Failure_Signals: frame rate drops below 30fps; blank tiles >2s after zoom; zoom controls unresponsive; console errors

Title: Map Marker Clicks
Preconditions: - Map visible with multiple location markers rendered
Steps:
[ACT] Click on a map marker rapidly
[WAIT] Wait for info window/popup to open
[ASSERT] Measure time from click to info window visible using performance.now()
[ACT] Close info window and click a different marker
[ACT] Repeat rapid marker clicking across 5 different markers
[ASSERT] Verify transitions between info windows are smooth
Assertions:
[MATH] Info window opens within 200ms of marker click
[STATE] No lag between clicking a marker and the info window appearing
[STATE] Transitions between different markers are smooth without residual open windows
[DOM] Info window content is fully rendered (name, address, hours visible)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No errors during rapid marker clicking
Failure_Signals: info window open time >200ms; multiple info windows open simultaneously; console errors; blank info window content

Title: Memory Usage - Initial
Preconditions:
- DevTools Memory tab open
- Page loaded but pickup modal not yet opened
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Memory tab
[ACT] Take a heap snapshot before opening modal (baseline)
[ASSERT] Note total heap size from snapshot summary
[ACT] Open pickup modal
[WAIT] Wait for modal to fully load (list + map rendered)
[ACT] Take a second heap snapshot
[ASSERT] Compare delta between baseline and post-open snapshot
Assertions:
[MATH] Memory increase from baseline to modal-open state is proportional to data loaded (rough guide: <5MB for 1-5 locations, <20MB for 50 locations)
[STATE] No retained detached DOM nodes visible in snapshot diff
[STATE] Heap growth matches expected data payload size
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No memory-related errors or GC warnings in console
Failure_Signals: excessive heap growth (>50MB for <50 locations); large number of detached DOM nodes in snapshot; console GC warnings
Cleanup: Close modal; allow GC to run; heap profiler state remains active until DevTools closed

Title: Memory Leak Detection
Preconditions:
- DevTools Memory tab open
- Page in stable initial state
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Memory tab
[ACT] Take initial heap snapshot (baseline)
[ACT] Open pickup modal — wait for full render
[ACT] Close pickup modal — wait for close animation to finish
[ACT] Repeat open/close cycle 10 times total (record snapshot after each close)
[ASSERT] Compare heap sizes across all 10 post-close snapshots
[ASSERT] Check for monotonically increasing heap growth pattern (indicates leak)
Assertions:
[MATH] Memory after each close cycle does not grow by more than 1MB cumulatively across 10 cycles
[STATE] No detached DOM elements accumulate across cycles in heap snapshot
[STATE] Garbage collector reclaims modal component memory after each close
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during repeated open/close cycles
[CONSOLE] No 'Cannot read property of undefined' or event listener warnings
Failure_Signals: memory grows >10MB across 10 open/close cycles; heap size never returns to near-baseline after close; detached DOM nodes accumulate per cycle
Cleanup: Heap profiler state remains active in DevTools until manually closed

Title: Memory Under Load
Preconditions:
- Long session simulation
- DevTools Memory tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Take initial heap snapshot (session baseline)
[ACT] Perform multiple searches inside the pickup modal (≥5 different queries)
[ACT] Open and close the pickup modal 5 times
[ACT] Switch between different pickup locations (click on 5+ different list items)
[WAIT] Wait 30 seconds after operations to allow GC to run
[ACT] Take a final heap snapshot
[ASSERT] Compare final heap to baseline
Assertions:
[MATH] Final heap size does not exceed baseline by more than 20MB after GC
[STATE] Page remains responsive throughout the session (no freezes or >1s hangs)
[STATE] No JavaScript out-of-memory errors
[STATE] Memory trend is stable (not monotonically increasing throughout operations)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No memory or performance errors in console
[CONSOLE] No excessive GC pauses reported
Failure_Signals: final heap >20MB above baseline; page freezes or hangs during operations; out-of-memory errors; monotonically growing heap with no GC recovery
Cleanup: Close all modal interactions; heap profiler state remains active in DevTools

Title: Network Request Count
Preconditions: - DevTools Network tab open and cleared
Steps:
[ACT] Clear the DevTools Network log
[ACT] Open the pickup modal
[WAIT] Wait for modal to fully load
[ASSERT] Count the number of API requests made during modal open
[ACT] Type a search query rapidly (type 5+ characters quickly)
[ASSERT] Count API calls fired during rapid typing
[ACT] Apply a filter option
[ASSERT] Count API calls fired for filter
Assertions:
[MATH] Modal open fires ≤2 API calls (e.g., locations list + optional config)
[MATH] Rapid typing fires ≤1 API call per query (debouncing confirmed — not per keystroke)
[MATH] Single filter change fires ≤1 API call
[STATE] No redundant or duplicate requests observed (same endpoint called twice with identical params)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Inspect request URLs and payloads for deduplication
[CONSOLE] No errors related to request failures or CORS
Failure_Signals: modal open fires >5 API calls; rapid typing fires 1 call per keystroke; duplicate requests with identical params visible; console errors

Title: Network Payload Size
Preconditions: - DevTools Network tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Open the pickup modal
[WAIT] Wait for all network requests to complete
[ACT] Inspect each API response in the DevTools Network tab
[ASSERT] Check Content-Encoding header for GZIP or Brotli compression
[ASSERT] Review response payload sizes for location list API responses
[ASSERT] Check for unnecessary data fields in the response payload (e.g., unused image assets, duplicate fields)
Assertions:
[FORMAT] All API responses use GZIP or Brotli compression (Content-Encoding header present)
[MATH] Location list response payload ≤ 100KB for ≤50 locations
[STATE] No obviously unnecessary data fields in location response (e.g., internal metadata not used by UI)
[FORMAT] Response Content-Type is application/json
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Verify Content-Encoding header on all JSON responses
[NETWORK] Check Transfer-Encoding for chunked responses on large payloads
Failure_Signals: response payload >100KB for <50 locations; no compression header on API responses; response contains obviously unused data fields; content-type mismatch

Title: CSS Animation Performance
Preconditions: - DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Start performance recording in DevTools
[ACT] Trigger modal open animation (click BOPIS pickup button)
[WAIT] Wait for open animation to complete
[ACT] Trigger modal close animation
[WAIT] Wait for close animation to complete
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Inspect the performance flame chart for layout and paint events during animations
[ASSERT] Check for use of CSS transforms vs layout properties
Assertions:
[MATH] Frame rate ≥ 60fps throughout open and close animations
[STATE] Animations use CSS transform and opacity (GPU-composited layers) — NOT width/height/top/left changes
[STATE] No forced synchronous layouts (layout thrashing) in flame chart
[STATE] No long tasks (>50ms) triggered by animations
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during animation cycles
Failure_Signals: frame rate <30fps during animation; layout thrashing visible in flame chart; long tasks >50ms during open/close; console errors

Title: Paint/Layout Metrics
Preconditions: - DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[ACT] Start DevTools Performance recording
[ACT] Interact with the page: open modal, perform a search, apply a filter, scroll the list
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Inspect paint events in the performance timeline
[ASSERT] Inspect layout events in the performance timeline
[ASSERT] Identify any tasks with duration >50ms (long tasks)
Assertions:
[MATH] No individual long tasks >50ms during standard interactions
[STATE] Paint operations are minimal — only affected regions repaint (no full-page repaints on filter/search)
[STATE] Layout events are scoped to modal content area (no full document reflow on search/filter)
[STATE] No repeated unnecessary layout/paint cycles
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No layout-related JavaScript errors
[CONSOLE] No 'Forced reflow' warnings
Failure_Signals: long tasks >50ms on search/filter; full-page repaint on every search keystroke; full document reflow on filter; console layout warnings

Title: Page Load - Category
Preconditions:
- Lighthouse extension or DevTools Lighthouse tab available
- Category page URL known
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page: {{FRONT_URL}}/category (or equivalent)
[WAIT] Wait for page to reach interactive state
[ACT] Open DevTools Lighthouse tab (or use mcp__Chrome_DevTools__lighthouse_audit)
[ACT] Run Lighthouse audit in Mobile mode with throttling enabled
[ASSERT] Record LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) score
[ASSERT] Record FID/INP (First Input Delay / Interaction to Next Paint) score
[ASSERT] Record CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) score
Assertions:
[MATH] LCP < 2.5s (Good threshold per Core Web Vitals)
[MATH] FID < 100ms (Good threshold) OR INP < 200ms
[MATH] CLS < 0.1 (Good threshold)
[FORMAT] Lighthouse Performance score ≥ 70
[STATE] All product images visible and not broken
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Verify no blocking render resources (large undeferred CSS/JS)
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during page load
Failure_Signals: LCP >4s (Poor); CLS >0.25 (Poor); FID >300ms (Poor); Lighthouse score <50; broken images on load

Title: Page Load - Product
Preconditions:
- Product page URL known
- Performance measurement tooling ready (DevTools or Lighthouse)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a product detail page: {{FRONT_URL}}/product-slug
[WAIT] Wait for page to reach interactive state
[ACT] Run Lighthouse audit or record Core Web Vitals via DevTools Performance Insights
[ASSERT] Record LCP value (primary image or product title)
[ASSERT] Record CLS value (check for layout shift from late-loading images)
[ASSERT] Verify images use lazy loading for below-fold content
Assertions:
[MATH] LCP < 2.5s (Good)
[MATH] CLS < 0.1 (Good) — product images must have explicit width/height attributes to prevent CLS
[STATE] Primary product image renders within LCP threshold
[DOM] Below-fold images have loading="lazy" attribute
[STATE] Page is interactive (add-to-cart button clickable) within 3s
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Verify product images are served in modern format (WebP or AVIF)
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during product page load
Failure_Signals: LCP >4s; CLS >0.25 caused by image reflow; add-to-cart button not interactive within 3s; console errors

Title: Page Load - Cart
Preconditions:
- At least 1 item added to cart before test
- Cart page accessible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to cart page: {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] Wait for page load and all cart items to render
[ACT] Record page load time using DevTools Performance or performance.now() at DOMContentLoaded and load events
[ASSERT] Measure time from navigation start to cart items fully rendered and interactive
Assertions:
[MATH] Cart page loads within 2s (time to fully interactive with all items rendered)
[DOM] All cart line items visible with correct product names, quantities, and prices
[STATE] Cart interaction controls (quantity +/-, remove, proceed to checkout) are all clickable within 2s
[STATE] No blank or loading placeholder states remaining after 2s
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Cart data API response received within 1s
[CONSOLE] No errors during cart page load
Failure_Signals: cart loads >2s; line items missing or showing placeholders after 2s; controls not interactive within 2s; console errors
Cleanup: None — cart state left as-is after test

Title: API Response Times
Preconditions:
- DevTools Network tab open
- User is on storefront and will perform typical session actions
Steps:
[ACT] Open DevTools Network tab; filter to XHR/Fetch requests
[NAV] Navigate through the storefront: home → category → product → cart
[ACT] Perform a search query
[ACT] Add a product to cart
[WAIT] Allow all API calls to complete after each action
[ASSERT] Review timing column for each API response in the Network tab
[ASSERT] Identify any endpoints with response time >500ms
Assertions:
[MATH] ≥90% of API responses complete within 500ms
[STATE] No API timeouts (no requests with status 0 or 504)
[STATE] No failed API requests (no 4xx/5xx responses) during normal session flow
[FORMAT] All API responses return valid JSON (no HTML error pages)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Flag any endpoint responding >1000ms as a performance issue
[CONSOLE] No network error messages in console
Failure_Signals: any API response >1000ms; 504 Gateway Timeout; 5xx errors during normal flow; HTML error page returned as API response; >10% of calls exceed 500ms

Title: Scroll Performance - Long Lists
Preconditions:
- Category page with a large number of products loaded (≥50 items visible or via infinite scroll)
- DevTools Performance tab open
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page with many products: {{FRONT_URL}}/category
[WAIT] Wait for initial product grid to render
[ACT] Start performance recording in DevTools
[SCROLL] Scroll rapidly from top to bottom of the product list
[SCROLL] Scroll back to top
[ACT] Continue scrolling for at least 5 seconds total
[ACT] Stop recording
[ASSERT] Inspect frame rate throughout scroll session
Assertions:
[MATH] Frame rate ≥ 60fps sustained during scrolling (no drops below 30fps for >500ms)
[STATE] No jank or visible stutter during scroll
[STATE] Product images load progressively as they scroll into viewport (lazy loading)
[STATE] If list exceeds 100 items: virtual scrolling or windowing is implemented (DOM node count stays bounded)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during scroll
[NETWORK] Check if infinite scroll triggers additional API calls at correct scroll threshold
Failure_Signals: frame rate drops below 30fps for >500ms; severe jank or scroll freeze; all product images load simultaneously on page load (no lazy loading); DOM node count grows unboundedly with scroll; console errors
